LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO HUMAN SERVICES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED BECAUSE DEFENDANT HAS NOT ESTABLISHED A RIGHT TO JUDGMENT ON THE ACCOMODATION CLAIM. AS TO THAT CLAIM THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO DEFENDANT'S KNOWLEDGE. IF DEFENDANT HAD KNOWLEDGE, DEFENDANT WAS OBLIGATED TO ENGAGE IN THE INTERACTIVE PROCESS. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE CLAIMS FOR DISABILITY DISCRIMINATION AND DISCRIMINATORY DISCHARGE. DEFENDANT HAS DEMONSTRATED A PERSUASIVE SET OF FACTS ESTABLISHING THAT PLAINTIFF WAS TERMINATED FOR POOR JOB PERFORMANCE. DEFENDANT HAS NOT SHOWN THE REASONS FOR THIS TERMINATION WAS PRETEXTUAL. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: (ON PHYSICAL DISABILITY & DISCRIMINATORY DISCHARGE CAUSES OF ACTION FROM GRANTING IN PARTY OF SUMMARY JUDGMENT) SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT
